setup.name: OculusThirdPartyTestDeveloper
setup.info:
Create an Oculus Test User for use by an external (non-employee) person, but owned/managed by a Meta Employee. These Users live in prod, and are used for testing third party apps. See https://developers.meta.com/horizon/resources/test-users/ for more info.

### Parameters:
?**username_prefix**: Username Prefix Prefix for the username. Must be provided for 3rd party users. (default: "oculus_test_")


?**email_prefix**: Email Prefix Prefix for the email. Must be provided for 3rd party users. (default: "oculus_test_")


?**password**: Test User Password If you don't provide a password, a random 12 character alphanumeric password will be used. (default: "")


?**org_name**: Org Name Leave blank for a random organization name. (default: "")


?**generate_app_and_binary**: Generate Unpublished App and Binary Mark to create a draft app submission and attach a test binary for the developer. (default: false)



### Returned Data:
**FBID** account_id

**string** email

**string** password

params.name: username_prefix
params.info: Prefix for the username. Must be provided for 3rd party users.

params.name: email_prefix
params.info: Prefix for the email. Must be provided for 3rd party users.

params.name: password
params.info: If you don't provide a password, a random 12 character alphanumeric password will be used.

params.name: org_name
params.info: Leave blank for a random organization name.

params.name: generate_app_and_binary
params.info: Mark to create a draft app submission and attach a test binary for the developer.

setup.name: WearablesTestDeveloper
setup.info:
Create a Wearables Developer Center test developer, owned/managed by a Meta Employee. These accounts are used for testing the Wearables Developer Center platform.

### Parameters:
?**user_prefix**: User Prefix Prefix for the generated company name and email. E.g. "mbbrc" produces "MBBRC Test Co <suffix>" and "MBBRC_test_<suffix>@tfbnw.net". Defaults to "WDC" if omitted. (default: "")


?**company_name**: Company Name Overrides the prefix-based company name. (default: "")


?**admin_name**: Admin Name Leave blank for a random admin name. (default: "")


?**email**: Email Overrides the prefix-based email. Uses @tfbnw.net domain. (default: "")


?**password**: Password If not provided, a random 10-character alphanumeric password will be generated. (default: "")


?**org_name**: Organization Name Leave blank for a random organization name. (default: "")


?**create_application**: Create Application Mark to create a wearables application for the developer. (default: false)


?**app_name**: Application Name Name for the application. Leave blank for a random name. (default: "")



### Returned Data:
**int** dev_profile_id

**int** mma_id

**string** email

**string** password

**string** org_name

**string** company_name

**?int** app_id

params.name: user_prefix
params.info: Prefix for the generated company name and email. E.g. "mbbrc" produces "MBBRC Test Co <suffix>" and "MBBRC_test_<suffix>@tfbnw.net". Defaults to "WDC" if omitted.

params.name: create_application
params.info: Mark to create a wearables application for the developer.

setup.name: WhitehatTestUser
setup.info:
Create a whitehat test user.

### Parameters:
?**name**: Name If you don't provide a name, we'll generate a random one. (default: "")


?**email**: Email If you don't provide an email, we'll generate a random one that's receivable by you. (default: "")


?**age_range**: Age range  (default: "adult")


**owner_id**: Owner ID If you don't provide an owner, we'll use the viewer context. (default: null)



### Returned Data:
**FBID** uid

**string** password

params.name: name
params.info: If you don't provide a name, we'll generate a random one.

setup.name: User
setup.info:
Create a Whitehat test user.

### POSITIONAL PARAMETERS
* Label: The label that will be used to reference the created object later in the FBDL script.
### EXAMPLE
Creates a User.

```
[setup]
  User UserOne
```

setup.name: Page
setup.info:
Create a Page with various setting options.

### POSITIONAL PARAMETERS
* Label: The label that will be used to reference the created object later in the FBDL script.
### KEYWORD PARAMETERS
* owner: The owner of the page
* ?is_parent_location: If set to true, this page will be marked as the parent location page. The default is false.Value: false | true
* ?admins: Users to add as page admins.
* ?editors: Users to add as page editors.
* ?moderators: Users to add as page moderators.
* ?advertisers: Users to add as page advertisers.
* ?analysts: Users to add as page analysts.
### EXAMPLE
Create a User and make that user an owner of the Page.

```
[setup]
  User UserOne
  Page PageOne with {owner: UserOne}
```

params.name: is_parent_location
params.info: Whether this is a parent location

setup.name: Group
setup.info:
Create a Group with various setting options.

### POSITIONAL PARAMETERS
* Label: The label that will be used to reference the created object later in the FBDL script.
### KEYWORD PARAMETERS
* owner: Owner of the group.
* privacy: Choose from public | private | public_legacy.
* visibility: Choose from anyone (default) | members_only.
* ?members: The list of members of the group.
* ?admins: The list of admins of the group.
* ?moderators: The list of moderators of the group.
* ?name: Name of the group.
* ?description: Description of the group.
* ?allow_anonymous_posts: Allow anonymous post setting of the group. Default is false.
* ?pending_participants: The list of pending participants for a public community.
* ?approved_participants: The list of approved participants for a public community.
* ?participation_control_enabled: Turns on/off participation controls for Public Communities. Default to false."
### EXAMPLE
Create a Group with an owner, an admin, two members, and set it's privacy to `private". In order to accomplished this, the required Users must be created before creating the Group.

```
[setup]
  User MemberOne
  User MemberTwo
  User OwnerOne
  User AdminOne
  Group GroupOne with {owner: OwnerOne, privacy: private, members:[MemberOne, MemberTwo], admins: [AdminOne]}
```

params.name: pending_participants
params.info: The list of pending participants for a public community.

params.name: approved_participants
params.info: The list of approved participants for a public community.

params.name: participation_control_enabled
params.info: Turns on/off participation controls for Public Communities. Default to false. Only for Public Communities.

setup.name: Album
setup.info:
Create a photo Album with various setting options.

### POSITIONAL PARAMETERS
* Label: The label that will be used to reference the created object later in the FBDL script.
### KEYWORD PARAMETERS
* owner: The owner of the album.
* type: The type of the album.Value: user | shared | page | group
* place: The place of the album. That is, where is this album from. Set to a User label for user and shared album. Set to Page and Group label for albums from page or group.
* ?privacy: The privacy setting of the album. The default is public.Value: public | friends_only | only_me | contributors_only | friends_of_contributors
* ?contributors: The list of contributors if it's a shared album.
* ?actor: The page that you wish to create the album as (owner still required). Also, only for group albums.
### EXAMPLE
Create a User and make that user the owner of the new photo album where the album's privacy setting is for friends_only.

```
 [setup]
  User UserOne
  Album AlbumOne with {owner: UserOne, type: user, place: UserOne, privacy: friends_only}
```

params.name: place
params.info: Where the album is posted (user, page, group)

params.name: actor
params.info: The page that you wish to create the album as

setup.name: Friendship
setup.info:
Create friendship between a User and a list of other Users.

### POSITIONAL PARAMETERS
* Label: None. This type does not require a label.
### KEYWORD PARAMETERS
* sender: The label of the sender (e.g.: a user)
* receivers: The list of labels for the receivers (e.g.: list of users)
### EXAMPLE
Create two Users and make UserOne be the sender and UserTwo be the receiver in the friendship.

```
[setup]
  User UserOne
  User UserTwo
  Friendship with {sender: UserOne, receivers: [UserTwo]}
```

setup.name: Business
setup.info:
Create a Business with various setting options.

### POSITIONAL PARAMETERS
* Label: The label that will be used to reference the created object later in the FBDL script.
### KEYWORD PARAMETERS
* owner: The owner of the business
* ?admins: The list of admins
* ?employees: The list of employees
* ?primary_page: The primary page that the business owns
* ?tier: The trust tier associated with the business (tier_[0-3])
* ?quarantine: Sets the business under quarantine
### EXAMPLE
Create an owner and two workers for this business relationship. Create a business Page and make the owner user as owner of the page. Create a business and make the owner as the owner of the business, add the two workers as employees of the business, and set the business Page we just created as the primary page for this new business.

```
[setup]
  User OwnerOne
  User WorkerOne
  User WorkerTwo
  Page BizPageOne with {owner: OwnerOne}
  Business BizOne with {owner: OwnerOne, employees:[WorkerOne, WorkerTwo], primary_page: BizPageOne}
```
Create and quarantine a business.

```
[setup]
  User OwnerOne
  Business BizOne with {owner: OwnerOne, quarantine: true}
```

params.name: primary_page
params.info: Primary page that the business own

params.name: quarantine
params.info: Whether business is quarantined (ADA integrity violation)

setup.name: App
setup.info:
Create an App with owner and set it's live status.

### POSITIONAL PARAMETERS
* Label: The label that will be used to reference the created object later in the FBDL script.
### KEYWORD PARAMETERS
* owner: The owner of the app
* ?live: Specify if the App is live (e.g.: app is published). The default is true.Value: true | false
* ?type: Specify the app's type. The default is none (Legacy).Value: business | workplace | consumer | instant_games | gaming | none
### EXAMPLE
Create an App with a User as owner and set it's live status as false. This means the app is not published.

```
[setup]
  User OwnerOne
  App AppOne with {owner: OwnerOne, live: false, type: instant_games}
```

params.name: type
params.info: Specify the app's type. The default is none (Legacy).

setup.name: Event
setup.info:
Create an event with various setting options.

### POSITIONAL PARAMETERS
* Label: The label that will be used to reference the created object later in the FBDL script.
### KEYWORD PARAMETERS
* owner: The owner of the event
* place: Where is the event.Value: Specify a label from one of these types: user | page | group.
* ?privacy: If place is a User, will set privacy for event. The default is public.Value: public | private
* ?is_past: If the event is in the past. The default is false.Value: false | true
* ?post_permission: Post permission setting. The default is admin.Value: admin | anyone
* ?post_approval: Post approval setting. The default is off.Value: on | off
* ?accept_invite: If the invited co-hosts are going to be accept automatically. The default is true. Value: false | true
* ?show_guest_list: Show the guest list. The default is true. Value: false | true
* ?co_hosts: List of co-hosts (e.g.: user or page).
### EXAMPLE
Create an Event for a Group where anyone can post and posts are approved automatically. To accomplished this, some Users and a Group is created first.

```
[setup]
  User OwnerOne
  User MemberOne
  Group GroupOne with {owner: OwnerOne, members:[MemberOne]}
  Event EventOne with {owner: OwnerOne, place: GroupOne, post_permission:anyone, post_approval:on}
```

params.name: place
params.info: Where is the event posted (user, page, group)

params.name: is_past
params.info: Whether event is in the past

action.name: make_anonymous_post
action.info:
Make an anonymous post in a health or parenting type group. Voice switcher is not supported here.
### SIGNATURE
```
       Subject as Subject make_anonymous_post Label with {group, approved_by}
```
### POSITIONAL PARAMETERS
* label: The label that will be used to reference the created object later in the FBDL script.
### KEYWORD PARAMETERS
* group: Label of a group object where the post will be posted.
* approved_by: Label of an user object who will approve the post when allow anonymous post are enabled in a group.
### EXAMPLE 1:
```
       UserTwo make_anonymous_post AnonPostOne with {group:GroupOne, approved_by: UserOne}
```

params.name: approved_by
params.info: Admin who will approve the post when allow anonymous posts are enabled in a group

action.name: make_post_text
action.info:
Make a text post as User or Page. Voice switcher supported.
### SIGNATURE
```
        Subject as Subject make_post_text Label with {place, ?text, ?tagged_object}
```
### POSITIONAL PARAMETERS
* label: The label that will be used to reference the created object later in the FBDL script.
### KEYWORD PARAMETERS
* place: Where to send the post. Acceptable values include label of User, Page, Event or Group.
* ?text: The text message with the post. The default is random text.Value: user provide or random text.
* ?tagged_object: The object being tagged (e.g.: label of a User or Page)
### EXAMPLE 1:
As PageOne, post a text with the specified text message to GroupOne.
```
        UserOne as PageOne make_post_text PostOne with {place: GroupOne, text: 'This is my first group post as Page'}
```
### EXAMPLE 2:
Post some random text to UserOne.
```
        UserOne make_post_text PostOne with {place: UserOne}
```

params.name: place
params.info: Where to send the post. Acceptable values include label of User, Page, Event, Group, or Fundraiser

params.name: text
params.info: The text message with the post. The default is random text

params.name: approver
params.info: Admin who will approve the post when post approvals are enabled in a group. Default will leave post unapproved

params.name: is_exclusive
params.info: Make post exclusive (paid) content. Default is false

params.name: audience
params.info: Specifies the audience for the post. Default to public

action.name: make_post_photo
action.info:
Make (status, photo or video) post as User or Page. Voice switcher supported.
### SIGNATURE
```
        Subject as Subject make_post* Label with {place, ?text, ?tagged_object}
```
### POSITIONAL PARAMETERS
* label: The label that will be used to reference the created object later in the FBDL script.
### KEYWORD PARAMETERS
* place: Where to send the post. Acceptable values include label of User, Page, Event or Group.
* ?text: The text message with the post. The default is random text.Value: user provide or random text.
* ?attachment_label: Specify the label for the media attachment. Default is '<PostLabel>Photo/Video'.
* ?multiple_photos: Specify if multiple photos are needed for make_post_photo action. Default is false when not provided.
* ?tagged_object: The object being tagged (e.g.: label of a User or Page)
### EXAMPLE 1:
As PageOne, post a photo with the specified text message to GroupOne.
```
        UserOne as PageOne make_post_photo PostOne with {place: GroupOne, text: 'This is my first group post as Page', attachment_label: PhotoOne}
```
### EXAMPLE 2:
Post some random text and a photo to UserOne.
```
        UserOne make_post_photo PostOne with {place: UserOne, attachment_label: PhotoOne}
```

params.name: place
params.info: Where to send the post. Acceptable values include label of User, Page, Event, Group, or Fundraiser

params.name: attachment_label
params.info: Specify the label for the media attachment. Default is '<PostLabel>Photo'

params.name: text
params.info: The text message with the post. The default is random text

params.name: multiple_photos
params.info: Add multiple photos in a photo post. Default is false

params.name: approver
params.info: Admin who will approve the post when post approvals are enabled in a group. Default will leave post unapproved

params.name: is_exclusive
params.info: Make post exclusive (paid) content. Default is false

params.name: audience
params.info: Specifies the audience for the post. Default to public

action.name: make_post_video
action.info:
Make a video post as User or Page. Voice switcher supported.
### SIGNATURE
```
        Subject as Subject make_post_video Label with {place, ?text, ?attachment_label, ?tagged_object}
```
### POSITIONAL PARAMETERS
* label: The label that will be used to reference the created object later in the FBDL script.
### KEYWORD PARAMETERS
* place: Where to send the post. Acceptable values include label of User, Page, Event or Group.
* ?text: The text message with the post. The default is random text.Value: user provide or random text.
* ?attachment_label: Specify the label for the media attachment. Default is '<PostLabel>Video'.
* ?tagged_object: The object being tagged (e.g.: label of a User or Page)
### EXAMPLE 1:
As PageOne, post a video with the specified text message to GroupOne.
```
        UserOne as PageOne make_post_video PostOne with {place: GroupOne, text: 'This is my first group post as Page', attachment_label: VideoOne}
```
### EXAMPLE 2:
Post a video to UserOne.
```
        UserOne make_post_video PostOne with {place: UserOne, attachment_label: VideoOne}
```

action.name: make_comment
action.info:
User makes a comment on an object. Mention is supported. Replying to a comment is supported. Voice switcher is supported.
### SIGNATURE
```
        Subject as Subject make_comment Label with {place, ?text, ?tagged_object, ?replied_comment}
```
### POSITIONAL PARAMETERS
* label: The label that will be used to reference the created object later in the FBDL script.
### KEYWORD PARAMETERS
* place: Label of an object where the comment will be posted. Supports post, comment, photo, and album.
* ?text: The text of the comment. The default is random text.Value: user supplied or random text.
* ?tagged_object: The object being tagged (e.g.: label of a User or Page)
* ?replied_comment: The replied comment. Must be a comment on the SAME post/object as place. (e.g.: label of a comment to reply to)
### EXAMPLE 1:
UserTwo is making a comment and tagging UserOne in the comment.
```
        UserTwo make_comment CommentLabel with {place: PostLabel, text: 'UserTwo is commenting here', tagged_object: UserOne}
```
### EXAMPLE 2:
UserOne replying to UserTwo.
```
        UserOne make_comment RepliedCommentLabel with {place: PostLabel, replied_comment: CommentLabel, text: 'That sounds great!'}
```
### EXAMPLE 3:
UserTwo is commenting on AlbumOne as PageOne via voice switcher.
```
        UserTwo as PageOne make_comment CommentLabel with {place: AlbumOne}
```

params.name: tagged_object
params.info: Object to tag in the comment (user, page)

params.name: replied_comment
params.info: Comment to reply to. Must be on the same post/object as place.

action.name: block
action.info:
As a User or page, blocks another user. Voice switcher supported.

### SIGNATURE
```
        Subject as Subject block Label
```
### POSITIONAL PARAMETERS
* label: The label of the user that will get blocked.
### EXAMPLE 1:
```
        UserOne as PageOne block UserTwo
```
### EXAMPLE 2:
```
        UserOne block UserTwo
```

action.name: remove_role
action.info:
Remove any permission a user/page has from a place. Support voice switcher.

### SIGNATURE
```
        Subject as Subject remove_role Label with {old_role}
```
### POSITIONAL PARAMETERS
* label: The label of the place that will have the user/page removed. Supports page role, group role, event cohost, album contributor and app role.
### KEYWORD PARAMETERS
* old_role: Label of a user/page to be removed.
### EXAMPLE 1:
Remove PageOne as cohost of EventOne.
```
        UserTwo remove_role EventOne with {old_role: PageOne}
```
### EXAMPLE 2:
Acting as PageOne, remove UserOne from GroupOne
```
         UserTwo as PageOne remove_role GroupOne with {old_role: UserOne}
```
### EXAMPLE 3:
Acting as PageOne, remove UserTwo from PageOne
```
         UserOne as PageOne remove_role PageOne with {old_role: UserTwo}
```

action.name: make_post_poll
action.info:
Post a poll to user profile, page, event or group.
### SIGNATURE
```
        Subject as Subject make_post_poll Label with {place, type, ?anyone_add_options, ?choose_multiple_options, ?tagged_object}
```
### POSITIONAL PARAMETERS
* label: The label that will be used to reference the created object later in the FBDL script.
### KEYWORD PARAMETERS
* place: Label of an object where the poll will be posted. Supports User, Page, Event, or Group.
* type: The type of the poll.Value: text | photo
* ?anyone_add_options: Specify if participants can add options. Default is true.Value: true | false
* ?choose_multiple_options: Specify if multiple selections is allowed. Default is true.Value: true | false
* ?attachment_label: Specify the label for the poll attachment. Default is '<PostLabel>Poll'.
* ?tagged_object: The object being tagged (e.g.: label of a User or Page)
### EXAMPLE 1:
Acting as PageOne, post a photo poll to GroupOne. Allow participants to add new options as well as allowing multiple selections.
```
        UserOne as PageOne make_post_poll PollPost with {place: GroupOne, type: photo, attachment_label: PollOne}
```
### EXAMPLE 2:
Post a poll to PageOne and do not allow participants to add new options but multiple selections is allowed.
```
        UserOne make_post_poll PollPost with {place: PageOne, type: text, anyone_add_options: false}
```

params.name: place
params.info: Where to post the poll. Supports User, Page, Event, or Group

params.name: attachment_label
params.info: Specify the label for the poll attachment. Default is '<PostLabel>Poll'

params.name: anyone_add_options
params.info: Specify if participants can add options

params.name: choose_multiple_options
params.info: Specify if multiple selections is allowed

action.name: make_post_violation
action.info:
Make a post with a certain violation.
### SIGNATURE
```
        Subject as Subject make_post_violation Label with {place, type, ?tagged_object}
```
### POSITIONAL PARAMETERS
* label: The label that will be used to reference the created object later in the FBDL script.
### KEYWORD PARAMETERS
* place: Label of an object where the violation is posted. Supports User, Page, or Group.
* type: Type of violation. See values list.
* ?tagged_object: The object being tagged (e.g.: label of a User or Page)
### EXAMPLE 1:
```
        UserOne make_post_violation PostTwo with {place: GroupOne, type: hate}
```
### EXAMPLE 2:
```
        UserOne as PageOne make_post_violation PostPage with {place: PageTwo, type: nudity}
```

action.name: add_cover_photo
action.info:
Adds a new cover photo to a user, page, group, or event's timeline. Supports voice switcher for a page.
### SIGNATURE
```
        Subject as Subject add_cover_photo Label with {place}
```
### POSITIONAL PARAMETERS
* label: The label that will be used to reference the created cover photo later in the results of the FBDL script.
### KEYWORD PARAMETERS
* place: Label of an object where the cover photo is uploaded. Supports User, Page, Group, or Event.
### EXAMPLE 1:
UserOne adds a new cover photo.
```
 UserOne add_cover_photo CoverPhoto with {place: UserOne}
```
### EXAMPLE 2:
Acting as PageOne, UserOne adds a cover photo to PageOne. Supports voice switcher.
```
         UserOne as PageOne add_cover_photo CoverPhoto with {place: PageOne}
```
### EXAMPLE 3:
UserOne adds a cover photo to the group GroupOne
```
         UserOne add_cover_photo CoverPhoto with {place: GroupOne}
```
### EXAMPLE 4:
UserOne adds a cover photo to the event EventOne
```
UserOne add_cover_photo CoverPhoto with {place: EventOne}
```

params.name: place
params.info: Where to add the cover photo

action.name: add_story
action.info:
Adds a new story to a user or page. Supports voice switcher to page via the as keyword.
### SIGNATURE
```
        Subject as Subject add_story Label
```
### POSITIONAL PARAMETERS
* label: The label of the Story that will be created.
### EXAMPLE 1:
```
UserTwo add_story StoryOne
```
### EXAMPLE 2:
```
UserTwo as PageTwo add_story StoryOne
```

action.name: change_post_audience
action.info:
Change the audience of a post.
### SIGNATURE
```
        Subject change_post_audience Label with {audience, ?friends}
```
### POSITIONAL PARAMETERS
* label: The label of the post to have its audience changed.
### KEYWORD PARAMETERS
* audience: The new audience setting.Value: public | friends | only_me | specific_friends | friends_except
* ?friends: A vector of specific friends of the post owner to either include or exclude in audience.
### EXAMPLE 1:
```
        UserOne change_post_audience PostOne with {audience: public}
```
### EXAMPLE 2:
```
        UserOne change_post_audience PostOne with {audience: specific_friends, friends: [UserTwo, UserThree]}
```

params.name: friends
params.info: A vector of specific friends of the post owner to either include or exclude in audience.

action.name: gen_token
action.info:
Generate an access token for Graph API usage (see https://developers.facebook.com/tools/explorer)
Use 'as' to enable page voice.
### SIGNATURE
```
        Subject gen_token Label with {app, ?scopes}
```
### POSITIONAL PARAMETERS
* label: The label that will be used to reference the generated token later in the FBDL script.
### KEYWORD PARAMETERS
* 'app': either FBDL generated 3rd party app or 1st party app from fb4a|fbios|fbwww|ig|bm|pct|.
* ?'scopes': default to public profile if not provided. must be a list of permissions.";
### EXAMPLE 1:
```
        UserOne gen_token TokenOne with {app: fb4a}
```
### EXAMPLE 2:
```
        UserOne gen_token TokenOne with {app: AppOne, scopes: [ads_management, ads_read]}
```

action.name: create_collection
action.info:
User creates a saved collection.

None

action.name: send_message
action.info:
User sends a message to another user/page/chat group. Supports voice switcher via the as keyword.

```
Subject send_message Label with {text}
```

* label: The label of the User to send a message to.

* text: The text of the message.
### EXAMPLE 1:
```
UserOne send_message UserTwo with {text: 'Test message'}
```
### EXAMPLE 2:
```
UserOne send_message PageTwo with {text: 'Test message'}
```
### EXAMPLE 3:
```
UserTwo as PageTwo send_message UserOne with {text:Hey}
```

action.name: share_post
action.info:
Share post as User or Page. Voice switcher supported.
### SIGNATURE
```
        Subject1 as Subject2 share_post label with {post, place, ?text}
```
### POSITIONAL PARAMETERS
* label: The label that will be used to reference the created object later in the FBDL script.
### KEYWORD PARAMETERS
* post: Post to be shared.
* place: Where to send the post. Acceptable values include label of User, Page, Event or Group.
* ?text: The text of the shared post.
### EXAMPLE 1:
```
        UserOne share_post SharedPost with {post: Post1, place: UserTwo}
```
### EXAMPLE 2:
```
        UserOne as PageOne share_post SharedPost with {post: Post1, place: EventOne}
```

params.name: place
params.info: Where to share the post (user, page, group, event)

action.name: add_page_task_access
action.info:
Give someone task access to a Page.
### SIGNATURE
```
Subject add_page_task_access Label with {new_role, tasks}
```
### POSITIONAL PARAMETERS
* label: The Page to add access to. This may be a setup-declared Page label, OR a dotted reference to an AP+ (Additional Profile Plus) delegate page produced by test_additional_profile_plus, e.g. MyAPPlus.delegate_page_ids.0. Use the delegate-page reference to grant a Limited Access Role (LAR) on an AP+ profile DIRECTLY in FBDL — this is NOT a manual browser step.
### KEYWORD PARAMETERS
* new_role: User to give access.
* tasks: List of tasks, choose from [content, messages, community, ads, insights].
### EXAMPLE 1:
```
UserOne add_page_task_access PageOne with {new_role: UserTwo, tasks: [content, messages, community, ads, insights]}
```
### EXAMPLE 2:
```
UserOne add_page_task_access PageOne with {new_role: UserThree, tasks: [insights]}
```
### EXAMPLE 3 (Insights-only LAR on an AP+ profile delegate page):
```
Owner test_additional_profile_plus MyAPPlus with {name: 'WH Cat 22'}
Owner add_page_task_access MyAPPlus.delegate_page_ids.0 with {new_role: Attacker, tasks: [insights]}
```

action.name: create_linked_group
action.info:
Creates a Group, add Page as Admin and/or add one or more Users as members of the group.
### SIGNATURE
```
        Subject create_linked_group Label with {?page, ?privacy, ?members}
```
### POSITIONAL PARAMETERS
* label: The label that will be used to reference the created object later in the FBDL script.
### KEYWORD PARAMETERS
* page: Label of the Page to be the admin of the Group.
* ?privacy: The privacy setting of the group. Default is private.Value: private | public
* ?visibility: The visibility setting of the group. Default is anyone.Value: anyone | members_only
* ?members: Label of one or more Users to add as members of the group.
### EXAMPLE 1:
Create a Group. Make PageOne as admin of the group and add UserOne and UserFour as members. The privacy is default to private.
```
        UserOne create_linked_group GroupOne with {page: PageOne, members:[UserOne, UserFour]}
```
### EXAMPLE 2:
Create a Group. Make PageOne as admin of the group and add UserOne and UserFour as members. The privacy is set to private and the visibility is set to anyone.
```
        UserOne create_linked_group GroupOne with {page: PageOne, members:[UserOne, UserTwo], privacy: private, visibility: anyone}
```

### EXAMPLE 1:

params.name: users
